items.title: Close anonymous data exposure on public GET routes
items.summary: CODE-VERIFIED 2026-07-05: handlers for /api/snapshot (build_snapshot: crew+activity+sessions+kanban+cron), /api/board, /api/chat/messages (attacker-chosen profile param), /api/files/list, /api/content/get do NO internal auth check — GET_PUBLIC_ROUTES is the only gate and they are on it. Anonymous callers read chat history and workspace files in any non-DEV deploy. SECURE_MODE origin check is header-based and does not stop direct requests. Also: _check_auth compares service bearer token with == (server.py ~L446) instead of hmac.compare_digest.
acceptance: Anonymous GET on snapshot/board/chat/files/content returns 401 unless deployment mode is single_user_local or DEV_LOCAL_UNSAFE
True-public set reduced to: /api/health, /api/meta, /api/setup/status, /api/install/status, /api/public/*, /api/auth/*, static assets
Bearer token comparison uses hmac.compare_digest
verify-public-deploy.sh asserts anonymous /api/snapshot -> 401
handoff_steps: 1. In server.py, split GET_PUBLIC_ROUTES into GET_ALWAYS_PUBLIC (health/meta/setup/install/public/auth/static) and GET_SINGLE_USER_PUBLIC (the rest)
2. In _is_public_get, allow GET_SINGLE_USER_PUBLIC only when deployment mode is single_user_local (read stack_config deployment_mode) or DEV_LOCAL_UNSAFE
3. Replace == token compare in _check_auth with hmac.compare_digest
4. Fix frontend callers that relied on session-less GETs (login screen must not call snapshot/chat before auth)
5. Add test_public_routes.py: anonymous GET matrix per deployment mode; wire into pnpm test:ci
6. Extend scripts/workframe/verify-public-deploy.sh with anonymous 401 probe

items.title: Split server.py monolith (definitive module map)
items.summary: ~2.6k lines remain in server.py (2026-07-08 @ 9538eec). ~50 modules + handler_modules/ mixins. Residual under 3k target met.
acceptance: Stage 0: characterization tests for model surface, auth gate, credential resolve (extends WF-034)

Stage 1 (leaf, no hermes deps): db_schema.py (Sprint G-K migrations, ~L19980+), auth_gate.py (GET_PUBLIC_ROUTES + _auth_check + session, ~L780-900), user_prefs.py (~L2000-4000), rooms.py (~L4000-6000), kanban_cron.py (~L10000-12000)

Stage 2 (hermes core): hermes_profiles.py (profile dirs/lifecycle, ~L6000-10000), profile_gateway.py (~L12000-14000), model_surface.py (hermes_models/model_set/billing, ~L14800-15800), provider_bootstrap.py (reconcile/auth pool/credentials, ~L12700-13060 + L19750+)
Stage 3 (transport): route_registry.py per WF-037; chat streaming + lane bindings module (~L13800-14200)
server.py residual under ~3k lines (main, wiring)

workflow.notes: 2026-07-08 @ 9538eec: server.py 2,602 lines / Handler shell 683; handler_modules/ six mixins; residual <3k acceptance met.

items.title: Single YAML config writer
items.summary: Replace hand-rolled line-based config.yaml editors (_write_fallback_chain, _set_profile_model*, _coalesce_profile_model_yaml) with one read-parse-modify-write helper. Root cause of the wizard YAML-corruption bug class.
sources: wizard config.yaml duplicate model: block incident (a7271ed)
acceptance: All model/fallback yaml mutations go through one helper
Helper round-trips comments or documents loss explicitly
test_profile_model_yaml.py extended to cover writer
handoff_steps: 1. Inventory all config.yaml mutators: _set_profile_model, _set_profile_model_provider, _set_profile_model_base_url, _strip_profile_model_proxy_fields, _write_fallback_chain, _coalesce_profile_model_yaml, _normalize_profile_config_yaml
2. Implement update_model_block(profile, **fields) using yaml.safe_load -> mutate -> safe_dump of the model/fallback_providers keys only, preserving unknown top-level keys
3. Replace each mutator body with a call to the helper; keep signatures so callers do not change
4. Extend test_profile_model_yaml.py: duplicate blocks, comments, missing file, concurrent double-write

workflow.notes: profile_config_yaml.update_model_surface is the single writer for model.default/provider/base_url/api_key/fallback_providers; coalesce uses coalesce=True; normalize keeps text-only pre-parse repairs (_fix_invalid_model_header, _fix_model_child_indent, _scrub_orphan_top_level_yaml_lines); comments not preserved on write (documented in module)

items.title: Model save/provider consistency regression tests
items.summary: Lock in 2026-07-05 fix: GET /api/hermes/models must not mutate model yaml; POST /api/hermes/model honors billing_provider; reconcile is idempotent; codex vs openrouter attribution correct when both connected.
acceptance: Test: repeated GET models leaves config.yaml byte-identical
Test: set model with billing_provider=codex survives reconcile + GET
Test: ambiguous model id (both providers connected) resolves to explicit/preferred billing provider
handoff_steps: 1. New test_model_surface_consistency.py using tmp WORKFRAME_API_DATA_DIR + fake profile dir with config.yaml fixture
2. Test A: call hermes_models() twice, assert config.yaml bytes unchanged
3. Test B: hermes_model_set(billing_provider='codex') then hermes_models(), assert provider==billing_provider=='codex'
4. Test C: _reconcile_profile_llm_for_user twice, second call returns False (idempotent)
5. Test D: gpt-* model with codex+openrouter connected honors prefer= current profile billing
6. Register in pnpm test scripts / CI

No user-visible behavior change

One provider-label helper across composer/settings/messages
Unknown model prefix renders model-only label, not guessed provider

items.title: Declarative route registry with auth requirements
items.summary: The BaseHTTPRequestHandler dispatch is a multi-thousand-line if-chain and GET_PUBLIC_ROUTES is a separate allowlist that can drift from handlers. Replace with one route table: (method, path, handler, auth_level, roles). Auth becomes deny-by-default at the table, closing the WF-031 class permanently.
acceptance: Single ROUTES table; unknown path = 404, unlisted auth = 401 (deny by default)
auth_level enum: public | session | role(owner_admin) | internal
Test asserts every /api/* handler is registered with explicit auth_level
GET_PUBLIC_ROUTES deleted
handoff_steps: 1. Define Route dataclass (method, path, handler, auth: public|session|role_owner_admin|internal) + ROUTES list in new routes.py
2. Write dispatch(handler, method, path, qs, body) that enforces auth from the table before calling handler fn
3. Migrate do_GET branches to table entries in batches of ~20 routes per commit (handler fns already exist — reference them)
4. Migrate do_POST/do_PATCH/do_DELETE the same way
5. Delete GET_PUBLIC_ROUTES and the if-chains; keep _handle_internal_llm_proxy/_action_proxy as pre-dispatch
6. Add test: walk ROUTES, assert no /api/* path reachable without explicit auth level

workflow.notes: ROUTES + ROUTE_PATTERNS; do_GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE dispatch via registry only; 55 GET + 44 POST + 4 PATCH exact; 62 pattern handlers; zero /api if-chains in dispatch; GET_PUBLIC_ROUTES deleted; test_route_registry.py audits all handlers + auth.

items.title: Prune stub packages and apps from monorepo
items.summary: CODE-VERIFIED 2026-07-05: packages/{core,domain,sdk,telemetry,testing,ui,workframe-core} and apps/{admin,mobile,worker} are 1-5 file stubs with no consumers. packages/{workframe,workframe-scoped} are near-empty BUT listed in pnpm-workspace.yaml — pruning requires workspace edit. NOT stubs: apps/website (real Next.js marketing site), packages/create-workframe (the product), gitignored operator trees (packages/contracts, packages/workframe-integrations). Repo tracks a clean 921 files; local-only junk (New folder/, screenshots/, runtime/) is untracked and just needs local cleanup guidance.
sources: audit-2026-07-05: file counts + pnpm-workspace.yaml + git ls-files
acceptance: Stub dirs deleted; pnpm-workspace.yaml lists only real packages
pnpm install + pnpm test:ci green after prune
AGENTS.md boundary rules updated if a named boundary disappears
handoff_steps: 1. grep imports repo-wide for @workframe/core|domain|sdk|telemetry|testing|ui|workframe-core to confirm zero consumers
2. Delete stub dirs: packages/{core,domain,sdk,telemetry,testing,ui,workframe-core,workframe,workframe-scoped}, apps/{admin,mobile,worker}
3. Remove packages/workframe and packages/workframe-scoped entries from pnpm-workspace.yaml
4. Run pnpm install; fix any lockfile/filter references (package.json scripts using --filter)
5. Update AGENTS.md / START_HERE.md boundary tables if they name deleted dirs

items.title: Domain model package: Cell, Agent, Run, Lease, Grant
items.summary: First-principles core: the stable Workframe layer is a small typed domain model (agents as persistent actors, runs as units of authority+economics, leases as scoped credentials, capabilities not ambient authority). Today those concepts live implicitly inside server.py SQLite calls and profile-slug conventions. Define them once — Python dataclasses + JSON schema — so API, UI, and future adapters share one vocabulary.
acceptance: Entities: Cell, User, Workspace, AgentIdentity, RuntimeBinding, Run, RunEvent, Lease, Grant, CredentialRef
Schema committed (packages/domain or services/workframe-api/domain/)
WF-NS-P2 runs tables and WF-009 RunAuthorityGate reference these types
Glossary in docs/public closes WF-NS-P0

items.title: Program roadmap: monolith → secure Hermes-native → agentic OS
items.summary: Definitive sequencing of existing backlog into stages. Stage A (stabilize): WF-031/034/033 + WF-006/019/020 release truth. Stage B (decompose): WF-037 route registry, WF-032 module split, WF-038 prune, WF-036 frontend. Stage C (secure multi-user): WF-026/027/028 broker layer, WF-025 forced egress, WF-011 supervisor negatives, WF-017 public hardening. Stage D (authority+ledger): WF-039 domain model, WF-009 RunAuthorityGate, WF-NS-P2 runs tables, WF-016 funding modes, WF-007 CellAuthorityGate. Stage E (platform): WF-013/014/015 + WF-NS-P4 adapter seam (second runtime), WF-NS-P3/029 tool brokers, WF-NS-P5/P6 cells. Stage F (business): WF-NS-P7 control plane, WF-NS-P8 marketplace + social/agent-to-agent economy. Principle throughout: vault and leases keep raw keys away from agents while terminal/web tools stay fully capable — brokered egress, not nerfed agents.
acceptance: Stage gates: each stage exits only with harness/evidence green
Backlog items carry stage labels or ordering derivable from this item
PM harness (operations/pm) picks next item by stage order
handoff_steps: Stage A (parallel-safe, one composer agent each): WF-031 (security fix), WF-034 (regression tests), WF-033 (yaml writer)
Stage B (sequential): WF-037 route registry -> WF-032 module split (one stage per agent run) -> WF-038 prune -> WF-036 frontend
Stage D: WF-039 -> WF-009 -> WF-NS-P2 -> WF-016 -> WF-007
Stage E: WF-013 -> WF-NS-P4 -> WF-015 -> WF-NS-P3/WF-029 -> WF-NS-P5/P6
Stage F: WF-NS-P7 -> WF-NS-P8
Every agent handoff: read item handoff_steps, implement, run pnpm test:ci + py_compile, update item status + patch_refs

workflow.notes: Closed: stage gates in program-status.md; program_stages in backlog.json; ledger-next picks B→C→D; PM runbook references program-status.
